message:
style: clippy sweep across codec, client, escape-parser, mux, term

Mechanical clippy output with no semantic changes. Nine files.

## codec

- `frankenterm/codec/src/bounded_varbincode.rs`: the `deserialize_bool`
  error path does `Err(Error::InvalidBoolEncoding(value).into())`.
  The trailing `.into()` is a no-op — the function returns
  `Result<_, Error>` and the expression is already `Error`. Drop it.
  (clippy `useless_conversion`.)
- `frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs`:
  - `test_frame_lengths` replaces the hand-rolled `let mut serial =
    1; for target_len in &[…] { … serial += 1; }` with
    `for (serial, target_len) in (1..).zip([…])`. Same set of
    serials, same set of target lengths, no state mutation in the
    loop body. (clippy `needless_range_loop`-adjacent.)
  - The rejection-shape assertion in the `ft-phz7x` cap test picks
    up an explicit `msg = msg` in its format-args so the captured
    format syntax compiles uniformly with the rest of the file.
- `frankenterm/codec/tests/proptest_codec_decode_fuzz.rs`: two
  `match … { Ok(_) => …, Err(_) => {} }` blocks collapse to
  `if let Ok(decoded) = … { … }` — no-op pattern, and clippy
  `if_let_else`-friendly.
- `frankenterm/codec/tests/proptest_codec_roundtrip.rs`:
  `prop_assert_eq!(decoded_json, payload.clone())` drops the
  clone; `payload` is not used afterward so the by-value move is
  fine. (clippy `redundant_clone`.)
- `frankenterm/codec/tests/regression_oom_crasher.rs`: rustfmt
  collapses the two-line `///   field reaches…` docstring onto
  a single continuation line (no content change).

## client

- `frankenterm/client/src/pane/renderable.rs`:
  `KeyCode::Backspace => { if self.cursor_position.x > 0 { … } }`
  becomes `KeyCode::Backspace if self.cursor_position.x > 0 => { … }`
  — the guard moves from the body into the arm pattern, so the
  "`x > 0`-else-do-nothing" behaviour is preserved but the extra
  `if` block goes away. (clippy `match_on_same_arms`-adjacent.)

## escape-parser

- `frankenterm/escape-parser/tests/proptest_kitty_apc.rs`: one
  `format!("Ga=d,q=2").into_bytes()` entry in the shape list
  drops the interpolation (nothing was interpolated) and becomes
  `b"Ga=d,q=2".to_vec()`. (clippy `useless_format`.)

## mux

- `frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs`: `backlog.entry(*pane)
  .or_insert_with(Vec::new)` becomes `.or_default()`. `Vec::new`
  is the `Default::default` for `Vec<_>`. (clippy
  `unwrap_or_default`.)

## term

- `frankenterm/term/tests/proptest_terminal_fuzz.rs`: two
  `proptest::collection::vec((0x80u8..=0xBF), 1..8)` /
  `(0xC2u8..=0xDF)` entries drop the redundant parens around the
  range (clippy `needless_parens`).

No public API, feature, dependency, or behavior change across any
of these files. `cargo clippy --all-targets` no longer warns on the
corresponding lints.

files.patch:
@@ -245,7 +245,7 @@ proptest! {
         let inputs: Vec<Vec<u8>> = vec![
             format!("Gf=24,s={width},v={height};aGVsbG8=").into_bytes(),
             format!("Gf=32,s={width},v={height};aGVsbG8=").into_bytes(),
-            format!("Ga=d,q=2").into_bytes(),
+            b"Ga=d,q=2".to_vec(),
             format!("Ga=p,i={image_id}").into_bytes(),
             format!("Ga=q,f=32,s={width},v={height};AAAA").into_bytes(),
             format!("Ga=t,f=100,s={width},v={height},i={image_id};AAAA").into_bytes(),
